choice: Enforce cross-household data isolation via database row-level security, using a logical household partition column on every household-associated table. Model users (identified by email) as having a membership association with households rather than belonging to them directly.
rationale: Human-chosen during grooming (overrode recommendation: Enforce in the application data-access layer for v1: every household-owned row carries a household_id, and a single mandatory scoping filter (active household ∈ the user's memberships) is applied centrally so no query can bypass it. Design the schema so Postgres RLS can be layered on later as defense-in-depth without data changes.)
